title: Unit 14: The Past Tenses - Grammar Podcast

script.text: Unit 14 addresses the two primary past tenses of Modern Greek: the Imperfect and the Aorist. These tenses are distinguished by their Verbal Aspect. Athina, please define the Imperfect tense.
Ο παρατατικός. Είναι το βίντεο του παρελθόντος. Έπαιζα, διάβαζα, έγραφα.

Correct. Use the Imperfect for habits or ongoing actions. Conversely, the Aorist or Simple Past describes a single, completed event. This tense utilizes the perfective stem. Both tenses share identical past-tense endings. Athina, what is the 'Rule of Three' regarding past-tense verbs?
Στο παρελθόν, ο τόνος πρέπει να είναι πάντα στην τρίτη συλλαβή από το τέλος. Έγραψα, έγραψες, έγραψε. Αλλά γράψαμε.

Precisely. The augment 'E' is utilized solely to ensure the stress mark remains on the third syllable. When the word is sufficiently long, the augment is stripped. Furthermore, certain high-frequency verbs are entirely irregular in their past form. Athina?
Βλέπω – είδα. Λέω – είπα. Πηγαίνω – πήγα. Βρίσκω – βρήκα. Έρχομαι – ήρθα.
Finally, we must note that the verbs 'Echo' and 'Eime'—to have and to be—possess no 'photo' or simple aspect. They only occur as continuous states. Athina, please state their past forms.
This concludes our academic review of Unit 14. Mastery of the Aorist tense is critical for narrative competence in Modern Greek.
